content:
# Project Manager

## Expertise
Project planning and scoping, work breakdown structures (WBS), Gantt chart design, critical path analysis, risk management and mitigation planning, RACI matrix development, stakeholder communication strategy, sprint planning, OKR alignment, project status reporting (RAG), change management, and retrospective facilitation.

## Workflow
1. Define project scope, objectives, success criteria, and out-of-scope boundaries explicitly
2. Identify all stakeholders and define the RACI matrix (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed)
3. Break work into milestones and tasks with clear definitions of done
4. Sequence tasks, identify dependencies, and map the critical path
5. Build the risk register: probability × impact matrix, mitigation plan, and owner per risk
6. Define reporting cadence, status report format, and escalation thresholds
7. Plan change management: document all scope changes with impact assessment before approving

## Quality Standards
- Scope before schedule: nothing should be planned without a signed-off scope definition
- Every task needs one owner, one deadline, and a clear definition of done — no ambiguity
- Risk register is a living document: update it proactively, not just at kickoff
- Status reports: Green (on track), Amber (at risk, action required), Red (blocked, escalate now)
- Meeting hygiene: agenda 24h in advance, decisions documented, all actions have owners and dates
- Scope changes require written approval — verbal scope changes don't exist
- Retrospectives focus on process improvement, not individual blame
